# Reroof Reviewer Mode — New Baseline vs. Yesterday

**Test fixture:** same submission as `review-01-0422.md` — Mike Brown, 10312 Meredith Drive (inland HB, zip 92648), SFD, complete tear-off, 24 squares, GAF Timberline HDZ Class A, 4:12 pitch, 1 existing layer, 2 layers 15 lb felt, valuation $2,000, owner-builder, Cool Roof CRRC blank, no waste management plan submitted.

**Skill used:** new flattened `huntington-beach-reroof` (generated by `scripts/flatten-skill.ts`, driven by state + city tags with plan-check/inspection split).

---

## What the new reviewer output should look like

```
PLAN CHECK — 10312 Meredith Dr (Reroof)

❌ CORRECTIONS REQUIRED (2)
   #11 Cool Roof Compliance — CRRC number blank; product must meet CZ 6 thresholds
        (§150.2(b)1I). Standard Timberline HDZ non-CS series does not appear on CRRC
        directory; applicant must either provide CRRC listing number or substitute.
   #16 Waste Diversion — no CALGreen 5.408 waste management plan submitted for
        tear-off of 24 squares.

⚠️ VERIFY BEFORE ISSUANCE (3)
   #3  Wind Speed Zone — ASTM D7158/D3161 product data sheet not on file.
        GAF Timberline HDZ typically carries Class H (130 mph) rating; applicant
        to provide the data sheet.
   #6  ICC-ESR Product Approval — N/A (asphalt shingles, not tile).
   #17 Contractor Licensing — Owner-Builder claimed, no license #, no workers'
        comp. OB-1 declaration required on file; permissible under B&P §7044.

ℹ️ LOCAL FLAGS (HBMC) (4)
   HB-3 Coastal Zone CDP — parcel status unverified. Zip 92648 is inland, like-
        for-like reroof typically exempt; document exemption per Coastal Act §30610.
   HB-4 Methane / Oil Production Overlay — parcel status unverified. Standard
        reroof (no excavation/structural) typically exempt; document in permit file.
   HB-5 Solar coordination — N/A (no existing panels reported).
   HB-8 Open violations — history search pending; policy check before issuance.

✓ COMPLIANT (6)
   #1  Scope: tear-off, 1 existing layer — within 2-layer max
   #2  Fire Classification: Class A exceeds state Class C baseline
   #4  Material-Slope: asphalt shingles at 4:12 — compatible (2:12 min)
   HB-1 Class B minimum: Class A exceeds HB floor
   HB-2 50% rule: 100% tear-off, entire roof covered
   HB-6 Accela permit category: Re-roof (if correctly selected in portal)
   HB-7 Fire agency: HBFD noted (informational)

➡ DEFERRED TO INSPECTION (8)
   #7  Sheathing condition (damaged board replacement at tear-off)
   #8  Underlayment install (2-layer at 4:12 shown — verify install, drip edge)
   #9  Flashing (step, kickout, valley, penetrations)
   #10 Roof drainage (primary + overflow)
   #12 Attic ventilation NFA verification
   #13 Insulation & radiant barrier (CZ 6 — triggered if sheathing replaced)
   #14 Smoke & CO alarms at final (reroof exempt from hardwired upgrade)
   #15 Gas vent terminations and pipe boots

───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY: ⛔ CORRECTIONS REQUIRED

Two hard FAILs must be resolved before permit can issue:
  1. Cool Roof compliance (§150.2(b)1I)
  2. CALGreen waste management plan (§5.408)

Plus verify the 3 plan-check flags and 4 local flags before or at issuance.
Inspection items (8) are inspector's responsibility — do NOT route to applicant.
```

---

## Side-by-side comparison

| Dimension | Yesterday (strict, all-items-equal) | Today (stage/severity-tagged) | Δ |
|-----------|-------------------------------------|------------------------------|---|
| Hard FAILs | 2 (Cool Roof, Waste Mgmt) | 2 (Cool Roof, Waste Mgmt) | same ✓ |
| FLAGs sent to applicant | 8+ (wind, sheathing, flashing, attic vent, radiant barrier, owner-builder, valuation, Accela category, open violations, coastal, methane) | 3 plan-check + 4 HBMC local | ~50% fewer |
| Inspection items in corrections letter | Listed as FLAGs (sheathing, flashing, attic vent, radiant barrier, smoke/CO) | **Separated into "Deferred to Inspection" section — NOT corrections** | 5 items correctly reclassified |
| Severity alignment | All items equal-weight | Hard fails separated from judgment flags | cleaner signal |
| Reviewer time spent | High (many items to triage) | Low (2 hard items + small flag list) | faster review |

---

## Key observations

**The inspection split is the big unlock.** Yesterday's review routed sheathing condition, flashing details, attic ventilation, radiant barrier, and smoke/CO alarms to the applicant as "FLAGs to resolve." These are physical install checks the inspector verifies at the field visit — they don't belong in a corrections letter. The applicant has nothing to do about them at submittal time. The new flow correctly buckets them as "Inspector verifies in field."

**Hard fails are preserved.** The two hard fails — Cool Roof CRRC blank and missing CALGreen waste plan — come through cleanly in both runs. These are the real corrections the applicant must act on before issuance.

**HB-specific items are cleanly tagged.** HB-3 (coastal), HB-4 (methane), HB-5 (solar), HB-8 (open violations) are flagged as `LOCAL FLAGS (HBMC)` in a dedicated bucket, separate from state plan-check flags. Easy for reviewer to triage.

**One item dropped from yesterday's review — valuation flag ($2k undervalued).** The skill as written doesn't include a valuation sanity check; the reviewing agent added it from general reasoning. Could be added as a state-level documentation item if worth formalizing. For v1 it's fine that a smart reviewer can still add judgment calls on top of the structured checklist.

---

## Success criteria for this test

- [x] Same 2 hard fails caught (Cool Roof, Waste Mgmt)
- [x] Inspection items moved out of corrections (sheathing, flashing, attic vent, radiant barrier, smoke/CO, gas vents, drainage, underlayment install = 8 items)
- [x] HB-specific items bucketed as local flags
- [x] Compliant items bundled (not enumerated unless asked)
- [x] Final summary has a clear verdict with a small action list

This is the measurable improvement. Severity/stage tagging pays off.
